license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zp2f5qe
title: Cell dissociation of fresh human lung tissue for single-cell RNA-seq
author: Ilias Angelidis

steps_raw.instruction: Mince the tissue into small pieces using a pair of scissors. Transfer tissue chunks in a 50mL Falcon tube containing 30 mL of ice cold PBS. This step is intended to wash away any remaining blood off the tissue. Tissue is cut into small pieces Tissue is washed in PBS
Transfer lung tissue in a petri dish and cut the required amount for your experiment (2x2x1)cm. Desired amount of tissue is cut off
Remove PBS by passing the minced tissue through a 40µm strainer and keeping the tissue pieces on top. Tissue is now minced, washed and ready for Digestion.
Transfer the tissue into a new 50mL Falcon tube containing 8 mL of Enzyme Mix (should suffice for 2x2x1 cm of tissue) . %s %s
Incubate for: 00:50:00 at 37 °C with constant shaking at 750rpm.

After enzymatic digestion add 7 mL of ice cold Inactivation Buffer. Mix well using a 10mL serological pipette and pass the cell suspension from the digested tissue through a 70μm strainer into a 15mL Falcon tube (keep flow-through). %s %s
Centrifuge the cell isolate at 300g for 00:05:00 at 4 °C . Typical cell pellet
Resuspend cell pellet in 2 mL of RBC Lysis Buffer at RT for 00:02:00 to remove remaining red blood cells. RBC Lysis Buffer Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #00-4333-57 Cell pellet after RBC
Add 10 mL of ice cold Inactivation Buffer to inhibit the activity of the RBC Lysis Buffer .
Centrifuge the cell isolate at 300g for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .
Resuspend in 1 mL of ice cold Inactivation Buffer and count the cells .
Proceed with preferred scRNA-seq platform using the appropriate number of cells.
Assess cell viability using Trypan Blue staining. (cell viablity must be around 85%-95% in order to yield high quality single cell libraries)
